items.title: Claude by Anthropic - Release Notes - March 2026 Latest Updates - Releasebot
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a significant start to 2026, launching major new Claude models and features. In February 2026, Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6 with a 1-million-token context window, stronger coding and planning capabilities, improved financial analysis, and the longest task-completion horizon (14.5 hours) of any AI model. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed shortly after, now the default for most users, closing the performance gap with Opus while offering near-flagship-level performance on coding, d

items.title: Claude Platform - Claude API Docs (Release Notes Overview)
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet:
Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Key model changes include the launch of Claude Sonnet 4.6, an improved balanced model with better agentic search performance and lower token consumption, supporting extended thinking and a 1M token context window (beta). Claude Haiku 3 (claude-3-haiku-20240307) has been deprecated with retirement set for April 19, 2026, with migration recommended to Claude Haiku 4.5.

items.title: Introducing The Anthropic Institute
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has made several major announcements in early 2026. On the product side, Claude Opus 4.6 was introduced as an industry-leading model for agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance (February 5), followed by Claude Sonnet 4.6 delivering frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale (February 17). Anthropic also introduced 'Anthropic Labs,' a team incubating experimental products at the frontier of Claude's capabilities, and published a new mo

items.title: Anthropic (@AnthropicAI) / X
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: Anthropic (@AnthropicAI) has been active on Twitter/X in March 2026 with several major announcements. Key highlights include the launch of The Anthropic Institute (March 11), a $100 million investment into the Claude Partner Network (March 12), and the release of the largest qualitative study on AI users featuring nearly 81,000 Claude user responses analyzing usage patterns, hopes, and concerns. Additionally, Anthropic announced an MOU with the Government of Rwanda — its first African government

items.title: Anthropic Economic Index Report: Economic Primitives
items.author: www.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has published several notable research papers and reports in early 2026. On the alignment and safety front, a paper titled 'The Hot Mess of AI: How Does Misalignment Scale with Model Intelligence and Task Complexity?' (February 2026) decomposes frontier reasoning model errors into bias and variance components, finding that as tasks grow harder and reasoning chains lengthen, model failures become increasingly dominated by incoherence rather than systematic misalignment.
